summary:
Adding sodium bicarb enhances local anesthesia efficacy primarily via faster onset rather than direct impact on drug penetration; its role is more about modulating physiological conditions for better uptake and action.
The addition of sodium bicarbonate accelerates the effects of local anesthetic agents mainly because it alters bodily fluids' acidity, enhancing their availability at nerve sites quicker while maintaining overall safety profiles similar to standard administration methods. To summarize concisely:

Adding sodium bicarbonate speeds up the onset and improves the duration of local anesthesia likely by affecting tissue pH, thereby optimizing drug delivery efficiency. This enhancement occurs within safe parameters typical for such procedures. Agent2 reasoned that although minor alterations may happen regarding how quickly ions move across membranes during application, primary benefits stem from improved accessibility post-administration into targeted areas under study involving regional blocks using lidocaine among others commonly used clinically today. 

Key Points Summarized In Two Sentences For Clarity And Precision Regarding Clinical Relevance Of Question At Hand Are As Follows :

Sodium bicarbonate hastens both initiation & prolongation periods associated specifically with locally applied medications improving patient outcomes efficiently yet safely ensuring no drastic shifts
